scope: analysis-only, no GPU, no training, no serving
hypothesis_tested: H2 from recipe_provenance_audit.md §6: regression-to-teacher on the gen stream (replay-stream training targets score materially below the new base's own raw completions)

inputs.note_on_task_brief_filename: task brief cited wp_bench_results_20260714_081354.jsonl for the raw-base anchor; that timestamp is actually the MERGED model's file (gen03_full/); used the correct raw-base-anchor file (082330) per gen_regression_forensics.md's own data-sources table

raw_base_knowledge_code_like_note: 0/320 raw knowledge answers matched a code/hook regex; raw answers to knowledge MC prompts are short identifiers only, never full code -- corroborates gen_regression_forensics.md's finding that raw (unlike merged) does not exhibit judge/code-format bleed-through
qualitative_pairs_verdict: 5/5 pairs show one-directional pattern: training targets are extracted, unwired, sometimes-undefined-helper-dependent class-method fragments; raw base's matched-task outputs are self-contained, self-registering, directly runnable units. One training row's own prompt text literally reads 'TODO: Copied from `section.php`.'

phpcs.confound: raw's higher total error count is dominated by Generic.WhiteSpace.DisallowSpaceIndent.SpacesUsed (488/814, 60%) -- a space-vs-tab indentation-character preference plus raw being 2.1x longer on average, not a completeness/quality defect. Training targets' top violation (Generic.WhiteSpace.ScopeIndent.IncorrectExact, 268 hits) is the mirror artifact of extraction (fragments indented at original nested class-method depth). Security-prefixed sniffs (nonce/escape) do point the same direction as the primary finding (23 vs 0) but are a small-n secondary signal, not the tiebreaker.

verdict_summary: Regression-to-teacher CONFIRMED for the gen stream. The 73 <wp_gen> replay-stream training targets are structurally and qualitatively below the raw new base's own output quality on every WPCS-relevant axis measured (hook wiring 8.2% vs 45.8%, <?php self-containment 8.2% vs 50.0%, docblocks 8.2% vs 29.2%), independently corroborated by 5/5 matched qualitative pairs and directionally by PHPCS security-sniff counts. This closes H2 from recipe_provenance_audit.md §6 and is independent, direct confirmation of gen_regression_forensics.md's Q5 finding (which inferred the same shape indirectly from the merged model's post-SFT behavior). H1 (86/13 judge/gen task-mix dilution) and H2 (sub-base-quality gen targets) are both confirmed, non-exclusive contributors.
target_quality_bar_for_rebuilt_mix: Open with <?php when the task implies a standalone file
Include the relevant add_action/add_filter/add_shortcode/register_* call in the same target when the prompt names or implies a hook
Do not reference $this->method() or free-function calls that are not either defined in the same target or an unambiguous WordPress core API

Self-distilling new targets from the raw new base's own outputs (Experiment 4, Claude-gated) is directly supported: raw already clears this bar at materially higher rates (45.8%/50.0%/29.2%) than the current replay stream (8.2% on all three axes)
